title: xerophyta viscosa
intro: Xerophyta viscosa, also known as the "sticky fig," is a plant species belonging to the family Velloziaceae, characterized by its sticky resin and unique habit of producing flowers and leaves on the same stem. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and fighting infections. Herbal preparations of Xerophyta viscosa are often made by infusing the leaves and stems in water or using them as a tincture, and are commonly used in traditional medicine in southern Africa.
